patches.rationale: WIN-012 is the magnetic-gravity coupling constant κ. The two-pole B(r) field equation described in this section belongs to WIN-053. Correct the section title to match the actual WIN being critiqued.

patches.find: WIN-012 presents a "two-pole geomagnetic model" as the dome's core achievement
patches.replace: WIN-053 presents a "two-pole geomagnetic model" as the dome's core achievement
patches.rationale: TLDR text incorrectly names WIN-012; should be WIN-053 (the two-pole field model).

patches.find: WIN-012 is presented as the model's core achievement: a "two-pole geomagnetic model"
patches.replace: WIN-053 is presented as the model's core achievement: a "two-pole geomagnetic model"
patches.rationale: Opening paragraph body text incorrectly names WIN-012; should be WIN-053.
IGRF-13 for degree/order 13 requires 195 Gauss coefficients (14^2 - 1 = 195). 168 is the count for degree 12 (13^2 - 1 = 168). Since the text says 'degree 13', the count must be 195.
